checklist.item: precompute_file_facts() extracts line counts via wc -l for all target files
precompute_file_facts() extracts heading counts via grep for all target files
precompute_file_facts() extracts code block counts for all target files
Pre-computed facts are injected into BOTH eval prompts AND research prompts (not just eval)
Eval prompt text includes explicit instruction to trust pre-computed numbers and NOT estimate or count manually
At least two additional pre-computed facts beyond line/heading/code-block counts exist (e.g., TODO/FIXME count, table count, word count, unique-entry count, reference file count)
In evaluate_criterion(), the prompt string starts with criterion ID and name before any file content or context
In evaluate_criterion(), the JSON output format and final scoring rules appear in the LAST section of the prompt string, after all context
In evaluate_criterion(), pre-computed file facts appear BETWEEN the criterion description and the final scoring rules
SKILL.md has a section or paragraph documenting the lost-in-the-middle problem and recommending rubric-first, decision-last prompt layout for eval_prompts
In evaluate_binary_checklist(), the prompt follows the same structure: criterion ID first, checklist items in middle, rules and output format last
Cross-model disagreement of 3+ points forces confidence to 'low' regardless of individual model self-reports
Confidence weights are conservative: 'low' confidence weighs at most 0.5x of 'high' confidence
Binary checklist criteria are automatically assigned 'high' confidence (no self-report needed for deterministic mode)
SKILL.md has a dedicated paragraph or section explaining that LLM confidence self-reporting is unreliable (overestimates by 20-40%) and that the cross-model disagreement approach is used instead

C8.name: Deterministic Pre-Flight Checks Before LLM Eval

checklist.item: Before calling evaluate_criterion or evaluate_binary_checklist, the code checks that all target_files actually exist on disk and skips or errors if missing
precompute_file_facts handles missing files gracefully with a FILE DOES NOT EXIST message rather than crashing
The eval prompt result parser validates JSON structure before extracting score (not just regex on raw text)
A fallback_score mechanism exists so parse failures reuse the previous score instead of defaulting to 0
The _parse_result function tries at least 2 different parsing strategies (e.g., line-by-line JSON then regex fallback) before giving up
evaluate_criterion() prompt does NOT include the full skill description or skill purpose — only the criterion-specific rubric and target file paths
research_criterion() prompt does NOT include the criterion name, score, or eval_prompt — it describes files objectively without knowing the improvement goal
improve_criterion() prompt does NOT include raw scoring language (0-10 scale, score X) — only reframed directives of what must be present
discover_criteria() prompt provides only existing criteria names and scores (not full eval_prompts) to avoid context bloat
Score stability detection exists: untouched criteria that shift 3+ points between cycles are flagged as [NOISE]
When dual-sample scores from two models disagree by 3+ points, the code uses a weighted average by model capability (not simple average or min)
The NOISE flag or stability warning is printed to stdout so the user can see which criteria have unreliable eval_prompts
SKILL.md documents eval stability as a concern and recommends rewriting noisy eval_prompts to be more deterministic or converting to binary checklist
Selective re-evaluation exists: after improvement, only criteria whose target_files overlap with modified files are re-evaluated (not all criteria every cycle)
improve_criterion() includes past failed attempts from results JSONL so the agent avoids repeating the same approach
improve_criterion() prompt includes an explicit rule to keep changes minimal and focused — not restructure or add unrelated content
improve_criterion() prompt includes a rule to preserve existing content quality (not delete good content to make room)
research_criterion() runs BEFORE improve_criterion() in the pipeline so the improve agent receives factual file state rather than assuming content
improve_criterion() prompt includes the current score so the agent knows the gap size and does not over-correct or under-correct
